description:
You are a system administrator who has been asked to verify that container-based
    web services can be built, launched, tested, and torn down correctly on the
    server.  Carry out the following workflow strictly from the Linux command line.
    The entire workflow can be completed with **no more than ten commands** (it is
    fine if you use fewer).

    1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/container_work  

    2. Inside that directory create two files  
       a) /home/user/container_work/Dockerfile  
       b) /home/user/container_work/index.html  

       Their exact required contents are:  
       • Dockerfile  
         --------------------------------  
         FROM nginx:alpine  
         COPY index.html /usr/share/nginx/html/index.html  
         --------------------------------  

       • index.html  
         --------------------------------  
         Hello from Container Ops!  
         --------------------------------  

       These files must be created **before** any container image is built.

    3. Build a local image from that Dockerfile and tag it precisely  
       localweb:1.0

    4. Start a detached container, naming it exactly  
       test_web  
       and bind host port 8088 to the container’s port 80.

    5. Verify that the container responds.  
       • Use curl from the host to request http://localhost:8088  
       • Capture the HTTP status code and the response body.

    6. Immediately write those verification results to the log file  
       /home/user/container_work/container_test.log  
       using **exactly** the two-line format shown below.  The automated test
       will read this file verbatim, so follow the format precisely (no extra
       spaces, blank lines, or additional text):

           2021-12-31 23:59:59
           HTTP_RESPONSE: 200 | BODY: Hello from Container Ops!

       Notes on the format:
       • Line 1 is the literal timestamp above – do NOT generate the current time.  
       • “HTTP_RESPONSE:” must be followed by a single space and the 3-digit
         status code returned by curl (must be 200).  
       • “BODY:” must be followed by a single space and the plain text delivered
         by the container (must be exactly “Hello from Container Ops!”).  
       • The pipe symbol (|) must be surrounded by exactly one space on both sides.

    7. After logging, stop and remove the running container
       (test_web) so that no containers remain active.

    A perfectly-completed task will leave the following artefacts on disk only:
    • /home/user/container_work/Dockerfile         (with the exact contents above)
    • /home/user/container_work/index.html         (with the exact contents above)
    • /home/user/container_work/container_test.log (exactly two lines, formatted
      as shown)

    No running containers, networks, or images other than localweb:1.0 should
    remain.  Automated grading will assert:

    • The three files exist at the exact paths given.  
    • The checksums of Dockerfile and index.html match the required contents.  
    • container_test.log contains exactly the two lines in the format shown.  
    • No container named test_web is running, and no other containers are active.  
    • An image tagged localweb:1.0 exists locally.

    Hints:
    • Any standard container runtime CLI available in the environment (docker,
      podman, etc.) may be used.  
    • Remember that most CLIs require elevated privileges only for installation;
      building, running and stopping containers as the current user is allowed in
      rootless mode.

    Run whatever commands you need, but keep the total count to ten or less.
    When finished, simply stop – there is no need to print anything extra to the
    terminal after the log file is written.
truth:
Required artefacts after successful completion:

    1. File: /home/user/container_work/Dockerfile
       Contents (exact, 2 lines, LF terminated):
       FROM nginx:alpine
       COPY index.html /usr/share/nginx/html/index.html

    2. File: /home/user/container_work/index.html
       Contents (exact, single line, LF terminated):
       Hello from Container Ops!

    3. File: /home/user/container_work/container_test.log
       Contents (exact, two lines, LF terminated):
       2021-12-31 23:59:59
       HTTP_RESPONSE: 200 | BODY: Hello from Container Ops!

    4. A local container image whose pretty-name (repository:tag) is:
       localweb:1.0

    5. Zero running containers (docker ps ‑q / podman ps ‑q returns no lines).

    No other requirements; grading will check SHA256 sums of the three files,
    verify image existence via the container CLI, and confirm zero active
    containers.
